name: Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path
name_original: Santiz desde Peñausende — circular por Teso Santo y El camino de los Contrabandistas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñausende offers routes through varied terrain, including historic greenways and paths alongside the Duero river. The landscape features rolling hills and traditional routes, providing a mix of natural and cultural exploration. This region is characterized by its rural setting, with routes often passing through agricultural areas and small villages. The trails provide opportunities to experience the local environment and its historical connections. Best mountain bike trails around Peñausende The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata, a 29.5 miles (47.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic greenway, offering a consistent surface for cycling. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.6 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including a hill and a historic path, providing diverse scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Duero Route and Sogo Bridge Loop from Pereruela, a 21.2 miles (34.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past specific landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñausende is defined by historic greenways, river valleys, and traditional paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on moderate routes. The routes in Peñausende are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñausende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails around Peñausende?
A: The mountain bike trails in Peñausende primarily feature moderate difficulty. Out of the 10 available routes, 8 are classified as moderate, and 2 are considered difficult, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Peñausende?
A: While most trails are moderate, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a historic greenway, offer a consistent surface that can be more accessible for those newer to mountain biking, though it is still rated moderate due to its length.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult-rated mountain bike routes available in the Peñausende area. These routes will offer more technical sections and demanding terrain.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in Peñausende?
A: The mountain bike trails in Peñausende vary in length and duration. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is about 29.5 miles (47.4 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 48 minutes. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282237388/hike-from-penausende-circular-route-via-teso-santo-and-the-contrabandists-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Peñausende – circular route via Teso Santo and The Contrabandists' Path&lt;/a&gt;, is 17.2 miles (27.6 km) long.

Q: Where can I park when mountain biking in Peñausende?
A: Specific parking areas are not detailed in the route descriptions, but trails often start and end near villages or accessible points. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for potential parking options in nearby towns like Peñausende or Pereruela.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails in Peñausende?
A: Peñausende is a rural area, and public transport options directly to trailheads may be limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to access the starting points of the mountain bike routes. Check local bus schedules for connections to Peñausende if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Peñausende suitable for families?
A: The region's trails are primarily moderate, which might be challenging for very young children or inexperienced family members. However, routes along historic greenways, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, offer smoother surfaces that could be more suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Peñausende?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on most rural trails in Spain, especially if kept under control. However, specific regulations can vary. It's always best to ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags. Be mindful of livestock in agricultural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Peñausende?
A: Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Peñausende, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters can be cool and potentially wet, though riding is often still possible.

Q: What interesting places can I explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: The Peñausende area offers several interesting sights. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy scenic views like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4420899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Salamanca Plains&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3019261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Cañada Real de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;. The settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4814729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cubo del Vino&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails in Peñausende?
A: As the trails often pass through or near small villages, you may find local bars or restaurants in towns like Peñausende itself, or other settlements along routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282165115/castillo-de-asmesnal-and-alfaraz-de-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt;. It's a good idea to pack snacks and water, especially for longer rides, as options can be sparse in rural areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Peñausende?
A: The mountain bike trails in Peñausende are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from several reviews. Users often appreciate the varied terrain, historic greenways, and the opportunity to explore the rural landscape.
tips: A good place to stop to rest a bit in the small rural town of Santiz is next to the Church of San Miguel Arcángel. There should be a water source nearby but check if it is drinkable or not.

An authentic living cathedral worth visiting. Near the town of Santiz, we find this hundred-year-old cork oak specimen, which is estimated to be around 600 years old. It is part of the Route of the Living Cathedrals
It is accessed through a metal door that is closed to protect the tree.
Beautiful gravel path that skirts the ridge until reaching the highest point of the Teso Santo, where the communication antennas are located. The trail continues following the wind farms.

The town of Peñausende is located in the Sayago region. On a rocky hill there are remains of an ancient settlement, where the old fortified castle stands, dominating the surrounding landscape and the commercial route.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel, Santiz
